question: I want to characterize client systems with Xeon processors network consumption, vs non-Xeon systems, further bifurcated by operating system. Provide me summary averages of total network consumption per client based on whether or not the clients are Xeon or not, with Xeon systems labeled 'server class' and non-Xeon system labeled 'non-server class', enumerate the based on the os the systems are running.

Provide me with a summary of the avg power consumed, the avg package c0, the average package frequency, and the average package temperature by chassis type. The report should contain these 4 metrics as well as the number of systems that entered into the calculation.
Provide me a summary of battery usage durations in minutes, broke out by the different cpu family and generations. Do NOT include any cpu family or generation with less than 100 clients.

Provide me a summary by country of battery usage in the world. For every country with at least 100 client systems, I would like to see the number of client systems, the average number of power ons on battery, as well as the average duration of time on battery.

Provide a statistical analysis of the different display connection types and the resolutions ran with those connections. Create a single resolution column as a concatenation of the resolution height and resolution width with a separator of a letter 'x' to separate the two values. The metrics of interest are the number of system, the average amount of time on ac power in seconds, and the average amount of time on battery in seconds. Round averages to 2 decimal places. Throw out any unknown values, and any grouping of connection type and resolution with less than 50 clients.

Provide a statistical summary of the various detected display devices. We want to calculate, as a percentage of the total number of systems, each vendor's display type has been seen at least once. Some clients will have more than one display; as such clients can be double-counted. The metrics to be provided are the number of clients per vendor, the total number of clients in the sample, and the percentage of the specific vendor's clients in relation to the total number of clients. Round the percentage off to 2 decimal places.

Provide me a summary count distribution of the number of top modern sleepstudy blockers, broke out by Windows os name and codename. The metrics to provide are the sum of blocker entries, the number of systems that produced those entries, and a number of entries per system for normalization purposes. Include only Os names and codenames for which there are at least 10 client systems.

Utilizing the web categorization usage data in conjunction with sysinfo geographic data, produce a list of the countries as the most popular browser by system count for each country. This will be a ranked list, so ties in an individual country should be allowed.

Provide me a statistical summary of on time, off time, mods time, and sleep time broke out by different cpu generations and Intel market codenames. Do not include any cpu generation/market code name grouping with less than 100 clients. The metrics to summarize number of clients, average on time, average off time, average mods time, average sleep time, and average total time per day, as well as average percent on time per day, average percent off time per day, average percent mods time per day, and average percent sleep time per day. Round all decimal metrics off to 2 decimal points.

Provide an analysis of web category duration usage, broke out by client persona classification. Provide the web category usage values in terms of weighted percentages, the sum total of which for any persona grouping should add to 100%. Provide the number of clients as well as the number of days of data that fed into the analysis for each persona.
Provide me a enumeration of the average cpu package power in watts consumed by a client, broke out by country.

Utilizing the web categorization usage data, produce a statistical analysis of the popularity of the browsers in that dataset. The metrics to calculate would be the percentage of total number of systems for which the browser was seen, the percentage of the number of instances for each browser, and the percentage of total duration used for each browswer.

Provide an ordered histogram of client memory capacity, showing the distribution of average utilized ram for each memory capacity column. The ascending memory capacity should be provided in GB (gigabytes) and the utilization should be an integer percentage. Filter out any data where the average utilized memory is negative, because that is indicative of erroneous data. Ensure the average computed is a weighted average, weighted with the number of samples.

I am trying to identify servers based on their network utilization. I believe that typically, servers will send more data than they will receive. To that end, please identify all client machines that are sending more data than they are receiving, utilizing the 'OS:NETWORK INTERFACE::BYTES RECEIVED/SEC::' and 'OS:NETWORK INTERFACE::BYTES SENT/SEC::' metrics in the reporting_system_network_consumption table. Note that to calculate sum total of bytes received and sent, you will have to multiple those metrics by the nrs column and by 5 to produce a data volume. Join the guids discovered to the sysinfo data to identify os, chassis, and vendor of the client systems meeting this criteria.

Provide me ranked lists of the top 10 applications by application type. Rank the applications by average amount of focal screen time the applications have a day. Ignore any applications with no application type classification, and allow for ties in ranking.

Provide me ranked lists of the top 10 applications by application type. Rank the applications by the number of distinct clients the applications have been detected on. Ignore any applications with no application type classification, and allow for ties in ranking.

Provide me ranked lists of the top 10 applications by application type. Rank the applications by the total number of detections seen per day across the application type, as measured by the metric lines_per_day. Ignore any applications with no application type classification, and allow for ties in ranking.

Provide me a summary count and duration distribution of the number of top modern sleepstudy blockers, broke out by Windows os name and codename, as well as the blocker name, blocker type, and activity level. The metrics to provide are the sum of blocker entries, the number of systems that produced those entries, and the average duration in seconds of these blockers. Discard any blockers which have a zero duration.

Provide me a list of the top 10 worst applications for wait time. Ignore the following list of processes in compiling the list: 'DUMMY_PROCESS', 'DESKTOP', 'explorer.exe', 'RESTRICTED PROCESS', and 'UNKNOWN'. Also ignore any events that are either 'TOTAL_NON_WAIT_EVENTS' or 'TOTAL_DISCARDED_WAIT_EVENTS', as they are not applicable to this analysis. Provide me a ranking number, as well as an average wait time in seconds for these ranked processes.

Provide me a list of the top 10 worst applications for wait time, segregated by type of wait (APPSTARTING) or in application (WAIT), as well as by the power state of the client. Ignore the following list of processes in compiling the list: 'DUMMY_PROCESS', 'DESKTOP', 'explorer.exe', 'RESTRICTED PROCESS', and 'UNKNOWN'. Also ignore any events that are either 'TOTAL_NON_WAIT_EVENTS' or 'TOTAL_DISCARDED_WAIT_EVENTS', as they are not applicable to this analysis. Provide me a ranking number, as well as an average wait time in seconds for these ranked processes.

Provide me a list of the top 20 worst applications for wait time, providing the average duration/instance PIVOTED by the power state. Ignore the following list of processes in compiling the list: 'DUMMY_PROCESS', 'DESKTOP', 'explorer.exe', 'RESTRICTED PROCESS', and 'UNKNOWN'. Also ignore any events that are either 'TOTAL_NON_WAIT_EVENTS' or 'TOTAL_DISCARDED_WAIT_EVENTS', as they are not applicable to this analysis. The final column definition should be the processes, the AC average duration per instance, the DC average duration per instance, and the UNknown average duration per instance.
